name: Grilled Steak Salad with Blue Cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. In a skillet over medium heat, toast cumin and coriander seeds until fragrant, about 1 minute. Place the toasted seeds in a Ziplock bag with peppercorns; crush with a rolling pin.
2. In a bowl, mix chili powder, garlic, and 4 tablespoons olive oil. Spread the mixture evenly on both sides of the steak. Let stand for 2 hours or chill overnight.
3. In a medium bowl, combine 6 tablespoons olive oil, vinegar, mustard, 1/4 teaspoon salt, and black pepper. Wash, dry, and tear lettuce into bite-sized pieces. Set aside.
4. Spray onion slices with cooking spray. Grill onion slices and plum tomatoes for 8-10 minutes, turning onions once and tomatoes frequently until charred on all sides. Transfer to a cutting board; cool slightly. Dice the onion and quarter the tomatoes. Stir into the vinaigrette.
5. Grill steak for 7-9 minutes on each side until medium-rare. Remove to a cutting board and sprinkle with the remaining salt.
6. Brush the cut sides of the bread with the remaining olive oil. Grill, cut-sides down, until toasted. Spread half of the blue cheese over the toast. Cut the bread into 8 pieces. Cut the remaining cheese into 8 thin slices.
7. Toss salad with warm vinaigrette. Divide salad onto 8 dinner plates. Slice steak thinly and arrange over salads. Top with remaining slices of blue cheese. Serve with toast.
